sections.paragraph:
διαρπάζω impf. 3 pl. διηρπάζοσαν PsSol; 1 aor. διήρπασα; fut. mid. 3 pl. διαρπῶνται Zech 2:9. Pass. 2 fut. διαρπαγήσομαι LXX; 1 aor. inf. διαρπασθῆναι 3 Macc. 5:41; 2 aor. διηρπάγην LXX; pf. ptc. διηρπασμένος LXX (s. ἀρπάζω; Hom. et al.; pap, e.g. PLond I, 35, 21 [161 b.c.] p. 25; LXX; PsSol 8:11; JosAs ch. 13 cod. A [p. 57, 24 Bat.]) plunder thoroughly τὶ someth. (Hdt. 1, 88 πόλιν; Diod S 12, 76, 5; Appian, Iber. 52 §220; Gen 34:27; Jos., Ant. 7, 77) a house (Zech 14:2; Da 2:5 Theod.; Jos., Bell. 4, 314) Mt 12:29 v.l.; Mk 3:27b; rob τὶ someth. (Hdt. et al.) Mk 3:27a (cp. IGR IV, 1029, 18f, restored thus: διαρπασάν[των]| δὲ καὶ τὰ [σκεύη τῶν ς]ωμάτ[ων]=slaves); steal ζωήν τινος someone’s livelihood Hs 9, 26, 2. Of pers. snatch away, abduct or take captive τινά someone (Diod S 13, 19, 2; ‘despoil’ Dt. 28:29; Ps 88:42; Mel., HE 4, 26, 5) IRo 7:1.—Mt 12:30 v.l.—M-M.

impf. impf. = imperfect

  1. pl. = plural

aor. aor. = aorist

fut. fut. = fut.

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

inf. inf. = infinitive

  1. pf. = perfect

ptc. ptc. = participle

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

PsSol PsSol = Psalms of Solomon—List 2

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

  1. ch. = chapter(s)

Bat. Bat. = PBatiffol, s. JosAs—List 5

someth. someth. = something

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Appian Appian , I–II a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Theod. Theod. = Theodotion, revision of Greek OT, II a.d.—List 2, beg.

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

IGR IGR = Inscriptiones Graecae ad res Romanas pertinentes—List 3

Hs Hs = Similitudes

pers. pers. = person(s)

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

IRo IRo = Ignatius to the Romans—List 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 235
